tasks.title: gx scaffolding: .zshrc block + CLAUDE.md carve-out
tasks.description: Register the gx git toolkit in two places. First, add a labeled '# gx git toolkit' block to ~/.zshrc that exports the path and registers all five verbs (gxcheck, gxpush, gxmove, gxclean, gxsync) as executable aliases pointing to ~/.dotfiles/.scripts/<verb>. Second, add a carve-out to ~/.dotfiles/.claude/CLAUDE.md (the single source file — ~/.cco/CLAUDE.md, ~/.cch/CLAUDE.md, and ~/.claude/CLAUDE.md are all symlinks to it, so one edit covers all). The carve-out goes under the shared scripts section and explicitly allows skills to invoke gx verbs via absolute path ~/.dotfiles/.scripts/<verb>, overriding the 'shared scripts only live in claude-code-shared/scripts/' rule. The .scripts/ directory already exists with pr-desc in it — no directory creation needed.
acceptance_criteria: ~/.zshrc contains a '# gx git toolkit' labeled block with PATH export or alias entries for all five gx verbs
After sourcing ~/.zshrc, `which gxcheck` and `which gxpush` resolve to ~/.dotfiles/.scripts/
~/.dotfiles/.claude/CLAUDE.md contains an explicit carve-out sentence permitting skills to invoke ~/.dotfiles/.scripts/gx* via absolute path
The carve-out is visible via all three symlink paths (~/.cco/CLAUDE.md, ~/.cch/CLAUDE.md, ~/.claude/CLAUDE.md) since they all point to the same file
No existing .zshrc content is modified or removed

tasks.title: Repo policy registry: claude-code-shared/resources/repo-policy.json
tasks.description: Create the per-repo policy registry at claude-code-shared/resources/repo-policy.json. The file is a JSON object keyed by normalized git remote URL (output of `git remote get-url origin`, normalized to strip trailing .git and trailing slashes, and stripping the protocol+host prefix so both ssh and https forms produce the same key — e.g. both git@github.com:Org/Repo.git and https://github.com/Org/Repo.git normalize to 'Org/Repo'). Each entry has shape {label, base_branch, exclude[]}. Populate three initial entries: (1) .dotfiles repo — key 'Eric-Lingren/.dotfiles', label 'dotfiles', base_branch 'main', exclude ['docs/seeds/', 'docs/tasks/', 'docs/handoffs/', 'docs/prd/']; (2) Quaestor-Web repo — key 'Quaestor-Technologies/Quaestor-Web', label 'quaestor-web', base_branch 'main', exclude ['docs/seeds/', 'docs/tasks/', 'docs/handoffs/', 'docs/prd/']; (3) SpawnedSapien repo — key 'Eric-Lingren/SpawnedSapien', label 'spawnedsapien', base_branch 'main', exclude ['docs/seeds/', 'docs/tasks/', 'docs/handoffs/', 'docs/prd/']; (4) ericlingren personal repo — key 'Eric-Lingren/ericlingren', label 'ericlingren', base_branch 'main', exclude ['docs/seeds/', 'docs/tasks/', 'docs/handoffs/', 'docs/prd/']. Also write a lookup helper in ~/.dotfiles/.scripts/gx-lib.sh that: normalizes the remote URL, looks up the entry, and falls back to a conservative default (base_branch: 'main', exclude: [], label: 'unknown') when the repo is unregistered. Unregistered repos must emit a visible warning to stderr.
acceptance_criteria: claude-code-shared/resources/repo-policy.json is valid JSON and matches the documented shape {label, base_branch, exclude[]}
Running the lookup helper from the .dotfiles repo returns label: 'dotfiles', base_branch: 'main', and the docs/* excludes
Running from an unregistered repo returns the conservative default and prints a warning to stderr
URL normalization strips trailing .git and trailing slashes so both ssh and https remote formats match the same key
Unit test (bats or inline bash test) covers: registered repo lookup, unregistered fallback, URL normalization

tasks.title: gxcheck verb: read-only branch state reporter
tasks.description: Create ~/.dotfiles/.scripts/gxcheck as an executable bash script. It is read-only — no git state is modified. It loads the repo policy via gx-lib.sh (T-0011) to get base_branch, then reports the following signals for the current branch: (1) MERGED: branch is fully merged into base (git branch --merged origin/<base_branch>), (2) FOREIGN: HEAD commit author is not the local git user, (3) STALE: local branch is behind origin/<current-branch> by one or more commits (git rev-list), (4) REMOTE_GONE: tracking remote branch no longer exists (git ls-remote), (5) ON_BASE: currently on the base branch itself. Output each signal as a labeled line (e.g. 'WARN: branch already merged into main'). Exit 0 always — gxcheck is informational, never a hard gate. Omit signals that do not apply.
acceptance_criteria: gxcheck is executable and can be invoked from terminal as `gxcheck` after sourcing .zshrc
On the .dotfiles repo with a merged local branch: outputs the MERGED warning
On a clean feature branch: outputs no warnings
On the base branch itself: outputs the ON_BASE warning
Exit code is always 0
Script sources gx-lib.sh for registry lookup — does not hardcode base_branch
Manual test: run gxcheck on the feat/gx-git-toolkit branch and confirm output is clean or shows expected signals

tasks.title: pr-desc stdout mode
tasks.description: Modify ~/.dotfiles/.scripts/pr-desc to support a --stdout flag. When invoked with --stdout, emit the generated PR description to stdout instead of (or in addition to) pbcopy. When invoked without --stdout, behavior is identical to today (clipboard only). This is required so gxpush --pr can capture the PR body via command substitution without depending on pbcopy. The --stdout flag must work correctly when pr-desc is called from a non-interactive subshell (the Bash tool environment), where pbcopy may not be available — when --stdout is passed in a non-interactive context, suppress the pbcopy call silently and only emit to stdout.
acceptance_criteria: pr-desc --stdout prints the PR description to stdout
pr-desc (without --stdout) behavior is unchanged: description goes to clipboard, success message printed
BODY=$(~/.dotfiles/.scripts/pr-desc --stdout) successfully captures the PR body in a subshell
pr-desc --stdout in a non-interactive shell (no TTY, pbcopy absent) does not error — it emits to stdout only
Manual test: run `pr-desc --stdout` from the feat/gx-git-toolkit branch and confirm PR description is printed to terminal

tasks.title: gxpush verb: preview-and-confirm push with flags
tasks.description: Create ~/.dotfiles/.scripts/gxpush as an executable bash script. Default behavior (no flags): stages tracked (modified, not new) files, commits, and pushes the current branch. Before anything leaves the machine, display a four-section preview manifest: (1) STAGED: files already in the index, (2) WILL ADD: files that will be added by this invocation, (3) EXCLUDED: files matching policy exclude[] globs (shown but not staged), (4) SECRETS: any file containing patterns matching common secret patterns (API keys, tokens, .env). Also surface UNSTAGED: modified-but-unstaged files that are NOT being committed. Prompt 'Proceed? [y/N]' — default is N (no accidental enter-to-push). Flags: --all runs git add -A before commit (blast-radius mode, explicitly shown in preview), --pick runs git add -p for interactive hunk selection, --pr opens a PR after push by calling `~/.dotfiles/.scripts/pr-desc --stdout` and piping the output to `gh pr create --title <branch-slug> --body -`. Loads registry via gx-lib.sh for base_branch and exclude[]. Excludes are applied as a filter: matching files are shown in the EXCLUDED section and skipped from staging even in --all mode.
acceptance_criteria: gxpush is executable and invokable as `gxpush` from terminal
Preview manifest shows all four sections before any git operation runs
Default mode stages only tracked files (git add -u), not new untracked files
--all mode stages everything including new files (git add -A), shown in preview
--pick launches git add -p for interactive staging
--pr calls pr-desc --stdout and creates a PR via gh pr create
Files matching registry exclude[] globs appear in EXCLUDED and are never staged, even with --all
Default confirmation is N — pressing Enter without 'y' aborts
Manual test: run gxpush --all on feat/gx-git-toolkit with at least one excluded path present, confirm excluded file does not appear in the PR diff

tasks.title: gxmove verb: relocate uncommitted changes to another branch
tasks.description: Create ~/.dotfiles/.scripts/gxmove as an executable bash script. It relocates uncommitted (staged or unstaged) changes from the current branch to a target branch specified as an argument: `gxmove <target-branch>`. Before acting, display a preview: (1) CHANGES TO MOVE: the list of modified/staged files, (2) FROM: current branch name, (3) TO: target branch name. Prompt 'Proceed? [y/N]'. On confirm: stash all changes (git stash), switch to the target branch (git switch <target>), pop the stash (git stash pop). If the target branch does not exist locally, offer to create it. If git switch fails (dirty index after stash pop, conflict), stop and leave the stash intact — print the stash ref so the user can recover manually. Local-only (unpushed) commits are NOT moved — gxmove only relocates uncommitted working-tree changes.
acceptance_criteria: gxmove is executable and invokable as `gxmove <branch>` from terminal
Preview shows FROM, TO, and list of files to move before any action
Default confirmation is N
On success: changes appear on target branch, source branch working tree is clean
If target branch does not exist: offers to create it before proceeding
On stash-pop conflict: aborts cleanly, prints the stash ref, and leaves both branches in recoverable state
Manual test: create a scratch change on feat/gx-git-toolkit, run gxmove to a test branch, confirm change arrived and source is clean

tasks.title: gxclean verb: on-demand merged branch cleanup
tasks.description: Create ~/.dotfiles/.scripts/gxclean as an executable bash script. It lists all local branches that are fully merged into the base branch (git branch --merged origin/<base_branch> | grep -v '^\*' | grep -v '<base_branch>'), with prototype/* branches flagged as priority sweep. Before deleting anything, display: (1) MERGED BRANCHES: full list, with prototype/* entries marked [PRIORITY], (2) count. Prompt 'Delete all? [y/N]' — default N. On confirm, delete each with git branch -d (safe delete — refuses unmerged branches). Report each deletion with its branch name. Unmerged branches are never touched. Also warn if the current branch is on the base branch (same ON_BASE signal as gxcheck). Loads base_branch from registry via gx-lib.sh.
acceptance_criteria: gxclean is executable and invokable as `gxclean` from terminal
Lists all local merged branches before prompting
prototype/* branches are visually flagged in the output
Deletes only merged branches (git branch -d), never unmerged
Default confirmation is N
Manual test: run gxclean on .dotfiles repo (known to have 14+ merged local branches) and confirm only merged branches are offered for deletion

tasks.title: gxsync verb: sync feature branch with main via merge
tasks.description: Create ~/.dotfiles/.scripts/gxsync as an executable bash script. It syncs the current feature branch with the base branch using merge (not rebase). Steps: (1) git fetch origin, (2) display what will be merged: 'Will merge origin/<base_branch> into <current-branch> — N commits ahead, M commits behind', (3) prompt 'Proceed? [y/N]', (4) on confirm, run git merge origin/<base_branch>. If git merge exits with conflicts, stop immediately — print the list of conflicting files and the message 'Merge conflict detected. Resolve conflicts manually, then run git merge --continue.' Do NOT attempt to resolve conflicts. Do NOT run git merge --abort. Leave the repo in the mid-merge state so the user can resolve and continue. Loads base_branch from registry via gx-lib.sh. Never uses rebase or force-push (respects block-destructive-git.sh).
acceptance_criteria: gxsync is executable and invokable as `gxsync` from terminal
Runs git fetch before computing ahead/behind count
Preview shows N commits ahead and M commits behind before merging
Default confirmation is N
On conflict: prints conflicting files and stops — does not auto-resolve or auto-abort
No rebase, no force-push at any point
Manual test: run gxsync on a branch that is behind main and confirm merge completes cleanly

tasks.title: Rewire outbound push in prototype, run-tasks, run-task-followups, debug
tasks.description: Update four skill SKILL.md files to call gxpush instead of raw git push + gh pr create. For each skill: (1) replace `git push -u origin HEAD` calls with `~/.dotfiles/.scripts/gxpush`, (2) replace the combined `git push ... && gh pr create ...` flow with `~/.dotfiles/.scripts/gxpush --pr`. Do NOT remove the user-facing confirmation prompts in the skill logic — gxpush has its own confirm gate, but the skills' 'Push and open a PR?' AskUserQuestion prompts are the user's decision point and must stay. The skills ask the user first; when the user says yes, they call gxpush (which then shows its own preview before acting). Skills affected: prototype/SKILL.md (the `git push -u origin` cherry-pick step for the PR branch), run-tasks/SKILL.md (the post-task push + PR flow), run-task-followups/SKILL.md (the summary push + PR flow), debug/SKILL.md (any push or PR creation steps).
acceptance_criteria: prototype/SKILL.md no longer contains raw `git push` commands — replaced with `~/.dotfiles/.scripts/gxpush` calls
run-tasks/SKILL.md push + PR flow calls `~/.dotfiles/.scripts/gxpush --pr`
run-task-followups/SKILL.md push + PR flow calls `~/.dotfiles/.scripts/gxpush --pr`
debug/SKILL.md any push references call `~/.dotfiles/.scripts/gxpush`
Skills' own user-facing confirmation prompts (AskUserQuestion) are preserved — they are the decision gate, gxpush is the execution gate
Invocation uses absolute path ~/.dotfiles/.scripts/gxpush, not alias, because skills run in non-interactive subshells where aliases are absent

tasks.title: gxcheck pre-flight in entry-point skills
tasks.description: Add a read-only gxcheck pre-flight to four entry-point skills: to-seed, to-prd-html, debug, improve-component. In each skill, insert a step before the existing branch creation / branch sanity-check step that runs `~/.dotfiles/.scripts/gxcheck` and surfaces its output to the user as informational context. The pre-flight does NOT block the skill — gxcheck always exits 0 and any warnings are advisory only. Branch creation logic in each skill stays exactly as-is. The gxcheck output should appear as a brief status block (e.g. 'Branch check: <output>') before the branch question. Use absolute path ~/.dotfiles/.scripts/gxcheck.
acceptance_criteria: to-seed/SKILL.md runs gxcheck via absolute path before its branch sanity-check AskUserQuestion
to-prd-html/SKILL.md runs gxcheck before its branch choice step
debug/SKILL.md runs gxcheck before its branch strategy question
improve-component/SKILL.md runs gxcheck before its branching strategy question
Pre-flight output is surfaced as informational context, not a gate — the skill continues regardless
Branch creation logic in all four skills is unchanged

tasks.title: Register all gx verbs in how-to skill (resources/commands.md)
tasks.description: Add a '## gx Git Toolkit' section to claude-code-shared/skills/how-to/resources/commands.md. Document all five verbs with their purpose, flags, and example invocations. Format consistent with existing sections (header, bold command labels, fenced bash blocks). Content to cover: gxcheck (read-only branch state: merged/foreign/stale/remote-gone/on-base), gxpush (preview + confirm push; flags: --pr, --all, --pick), gxmove <target-branch> (relocate uncommitted changes), gxclean (list and delete merged local branches), gxsync (fetch + merge origin/<base_branch> into current branch). Note that all verbs are invoked via absolute path ~/.dotfiles/.scripts/<verb> from skills.
acceptance_criteria: commands.md contains a '## gx Git Toolkit' section
All five verbs are documented with purpose and flags
Each entry includes a fenced bash example
The how-to skill can match a query like 'how do I push a PR' and return the gxpush entry
No existing commands.md sections are modified or removed

follow_ups.title: Add repo-policy.json entries for new repos as encountered
steps: When starting work in a new repo, run: git remote get-url origin
Normalize the URL: strip trailing .git and trailing slashes
Adjust base_branch and exclude[] to match the repo's actual defaults
Commit the updated registry to the .dotfiles repo on the current branch and push

follow_ups.title: Exercise all five gx verbs end-to-end after build
steps: After all five verbs are built (T-0012 through T-0017 done), reload shell: source ~/.zshrc
On the .dotfiles repo: run gxcheck and confirm output matches current branch state
Run gxpush on a branch with at least one excluded path present and confirm excluded file does not stage
Run gxclean and confirm the 14+ merged local branches in .dotfiles are offered for cleanup
Create a scratch change, run gxmove to a new branch, confirm relocation succeeded
Run gxsync on a branch behind main and confirm merge completes
Run gxpush --pr on a real feature branch and confirm PR is created with pr-desc body
